steps.title: Map the three parts of the text
steps.content: The text opens by naming Brisbane as a city that installed hardening structures (a concrete example). It then describes how Voss et al. used the Index of Waterbird Community Integrity to survey bird communities in Delaware Bay (the methodology of a study). It ends by stating the researchers’ conclusion that shoreline hardening harms birds more than land development does (the finding).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong descriptions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — It introduces a certain technique, mentions the hypothesis of a study into an ecological consequence of that technique, and presents evidence in support of that study's hypothesis.: The text ends with a conclusion, not evidence supporting a hypothesis, and the study’s conclusion is never framed as a hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice B — It provides an example of a location that has adopted a particular approach, explains the methodology of a study into an ecological effect of that approach, and describes a finding of that study.: Brisbane (example), the Index of Waterbird Community Integrity survey (methodology), and the researchers’ conclusion (finding) map precisely onto each part of the text. ✓
  • Choice C — It makes a claim about the use of a specific strategy, describes field observations of a consequence of that strategy in a particular ecosystem, and makes a supposition based on those observations.: The researchers reach a conclusion, not a supposition, and their work is a structured study, not informal field observations. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents a solution to a commonly occurring problem, summarizes the procedures used by a group of researchers studying the environmental impact of that solution, and notes the significance of the researchers' findings.: Shoreline hardening is presented as an existing practice, not framed as a solution to a problem, and the text states the finding itself rather than commenting on its significance. ✗
B is correct.
